description: Tektronix MSO3000 / DPO3000 Series mixed-signal and digital-phosphor oscilloscopes (100 MHz to 500 MHz, 2 or 4 analog channels; MSO models add 16 digital channels). Remotely programmed using Tektronix codes and formats conforming to IEEE 488.2 over USB, LAN (VXI-11) or the optional GPIB adapter. Mnemonics follow the Tektronix short-form convention (uppercase letters are required, lowercase are optional). <x> denotes a numeric suffix such as a channel, bus or search number.

identity.description: Standard IEEE 488.2 identification query. Returns a string such as 'TEKTRONIX,MSO3054,<serial>,CF:91.1CT FV:<firmware>' identifying the manufacturer, model, serial number and firmware version.

commands.name: acquire
commands.description: Returns the acquisition parameters

commands.name: acquire_magnivu
commands.description: Sets or returns the MagniVu feature

commands.name: acquire_maxsamplerate
commands.description: Returns the maximum real-time sample rate

commands.name: acquire_mode
commands.description: Sets or returns the acquisition mode

commands.name: acquire_numacq
commands.description: Returns the number of acquisitions that have occurred

commands.name: acquire_numavg
commands.description: Sets or returns the number of acquisitions for an averaged waveform

commands.name: acquire_numenv
commands.description: This command controls the number of envelopes (when acquisition mode has been set to ENVelope using ACQuire:MODe). The number of envelopes can be set from 1 to 2000 in increments of 1, or to INFInite.

commands.name: acquire_state
commands.description: Starts or stops the acquisition system

commands.name: acquire_stopafter
commands.description: Sets or returns whether the acquisition is continuous or single sequence

commands.name: alias
commands.description: Sets or returns the alias state

commands.name: alias_catalog

commands.name: alias_define

commands.description: Removes a specified alias

commands.name: alias_delete_all
commands.description: Deletes all existing aliases

commands.name: alias_delete_name
commands.description: Removes a specified alias

commands.name: alias_state
commands.description: Sets or returns the alias state

commands.name: bus_bx_rs232_c_tx_source
commands.description: Sets or returns the RS-232 TX Source BUS:B<x>:SPI{:CLOCK|:SCLK}:POLARity Sets or returns the SPI SCLK polarity BUS:B<x>:SPI{:CLOCK|:SCLK}:SOUrce Sets or returns the SPI SCLK source BUS:B<x>:SPI:DATA{:IN|:MISO}:POLARity Sets or returns the SPI MISO polarity BUS:B<x>:SPI:DATA{:IN|:MISO}:SOUrce Sets or returns the SPI MISO source

commands.name: bus_bx_state
commands.description: Turns the specified bus on and off

commands.name: cal
commands.description: Instructs the oscilloscope to perform self-calibration and returns the oscilloscope self calibration status

commands.name: calibrate_factory_status
commands.description: Returns the factory calibration status value saved in nonvolatile memory

commands.description: Returns the current status of the internal signal path calibration

commands.description: Returns the status of internal and factory calibration

commands.name: calibrate_results_spc
commands.description: Returns the results of the last SPC operation

commands.name: diag_loop_option
commands.description: Sets the self-test loop option
examples: DIAG:LOOP:OPTION ONCE runs one loop of self tests

commands.name: diag_loop_option_ntimes
commands.description: Sets the self-test loop option to run N times

commands.description: Stops the self-test at the end of the current loop

commands.description: Returns the internal results log from the last self-test sequence execution

commands.description: Selects one of the available self-test areas

commands.name: diag_state
commands.description: Sets the oscilloscope operating state

commands.name: diag_select

commands.name: configuration_advmath
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the advanced math feature is present.

commands.name: configuration_analog_gndcplg
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the ground coupling feature for analog channels is present.

commands.name: configuration_analog_maxbandwidth
commands.description: This query returns the maximum bandwidth for analog channels.

commands.name: maxsamplerate
commands.description: for analog channels.

commands.name: configuration_analog_numchannels

commands.name: configuration_analog_vertinvert

commands.name: configuration_applications_limitmask
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the optional mask/limit test application feature is present. As the MSO/DPO3000 Series oscilloscopes do not support this feature, this query always returns 0.

commands.name: configuration_applications_power
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the optional power application feature is present.

commands.name: configuration_auxin
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the instrument has an auxiliary input.

commands.name: configuration_buswaveforms_audio
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the optional audio bus triggering and analysis feature is present.

commands.name: configuration_buswaveforms_can
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the optional CAN bus triggering and analysis feature is present.

commands.name: configuration_buswaveforms_i2_c
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the optional I2C bus triggering and analysis feature is present.

commands.name: configuration_buswaveforms_lin
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the optional LIN bus triggering and analysis feature is present.

commands.name: configuration_buswaveforms_rs232
commands.description: This query returns a boolean value to indicate whether the optional RS232 bus triggering and analysis feature is present.

commands.name: application_license_slotx_location

commands.name: autoset
commands.description: Sets the vertical, horizontal, and trigger controls of the oscilloscope to automatically acquire and display the appropriate waveform(s). This is equivalent to pressing the front panel Autoset button

commands.name: autoset_enable
commands.description: Enables or disables the autoset feature
examples: AUTOSET:ENABLE 1 enables the front-panel Autoset button

commands.description: Clears the current menu from the display

commands.name: date
commands.description: Sets or returns the date displayed by the oscilloscope

commands.name: ddt
commands.description: Sets or returns the commands that will be executed by the group execute trigger

commands.name: fpanel_hold
commands.description: This command is used to emulate the button push-and-hold feature. Presently, only the Cursors button is supported by this command.

commands.name: fpanel_press
commands.description: Simulates the action of pressing a specified front-panel button

commands.name: fpanel_turn
commands.description: Duplicates the action of turning a specified front-panel control knob

examples: LOCK ALL locks the front-panel controls

commands.name: lrn

commands.name: recall_waveform
commands.description: This command (no query form) recalls a stored waveform to a reference location. Only the first waveform in a .CSV file is recalled for multiple waveform .CSV files. Recall of digital waveforms (D0 through D15) is not supported.

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_frameid_value
commands.description: when searching on the FlexRay bus frame This command specifies the low value to use ID field.

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_address_mode
commands.description: or 10-Bit Sets or returns the I2C address mode to 7

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_address_type
commands.description: special addresses Sets or returns the I2C address type to I2C

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_address_value
commands.description: be used for I2C search Sets or returns the binary address string to

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_data_value
commands.description: used for I2C search Sets or returns the binary data string to be

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_lin_data_hivalue
commands.description: used for LIN searches if the search condition Sets or returns the binary data string to be is ID or IDANDDATA

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_lin_data_value
commands.description: for a LIN search Sets or returns the binary data string used

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_lin_identifier_value
commands.description: used for LIN search Sets or returns the binary address string

commands.name: search_searchx_trigger_a_bus_bx_mil1553_b_data_value
commands.description: condition is set to DATa, this command When the MIL-STD-1553 bus search specifies the data binary pattern to be used in the search.

commands.name: allev
commands.description: Returns all events and their messages

commands.name: cls
commands.description: Clears status

commands.name: ese
commands.description: Sets or returns the bits in the Event Status Enable Register

commands.name: esr
commands.description: Returns the contents of the Standard Event Status Register

commands.name: evmsg
commands.description: Returns event code, message from the event queue

commands.name: evqty
commands.description: Return number of events in the event queue

commands.name: opc
commands.description: Generates the operation complete message in the standard event status register when all pending operations are finished Or returns "1" when all current operations are finished

commands.name: psc
commands.description: Sets or returns the power on status flag

commands.name: pud
commands.description: Sets or returns a string of protected user data

commands.name: rst
commands.description: Resets the oscilloscope to factory default settings

commands.name: sre
commands.description: Sets or returns the bits in the Service Request Enable Register

commands.name: stb
commands.description: Returns the contents of the Status Byte Register

commands.name: wai
commands.description: Prevents the oscilloscope from executing further commands until all pending operations finish

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_audio_data_hivalue
commands.description: This command sets the upper word value to be used when triggering on audio bus data.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_audio_data_offset
commands.description: This command sets the data offset value to be used when triggering on audio bus data.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_audio_data_value
commands.description: This command sets the lower word value to be used when triggering on audio bus data.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_can_data_value
commands.description: This command sets the binary data value to be used when triggering on CAN bus data.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_cyclecount_hivalue
commands.description: This command specifies the high value when triggering on the FlexRay bus cycle count field.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_cyclecount_value
commands.description: This command specifies the low value when triggering on the FlexRay bus cycle count field.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_data_hivalue
commands.description: This command specifies the high value when triggering on the FlexRay bus data field.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_data_offset
commands.description: This command specifies the offset of the data string, to use, in bytes, when triggering on the FlexRay bus data field.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_data_value
commands.description: This command specifies the low value when triggering on the FlexRay bus data field.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_frameid_hivalue
commands.description: This command specifies the binary data string to be used for FlexRay frame ID high value

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_flexray_frameid_value
commands.description: This command specifies the binary data string to be used for FlexRay frame ID low value

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_lin_data_hivalue
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary data string to be used for LIN trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_lin_data_value
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary data string

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_lin_identifier_value
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary address string used for LIN trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_address_mode
commands.description: Sets or returns the I2C address mode to 7 or 10-bit

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_address_type
commands.description: Sets or returns the I2C address type to USER

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_address_value
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary address string used for the I2C trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_i2_c_data_value
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary data string used for I2C triggering

commands.name: hivalue
commands.description: bus trigger condition is set to COMMAND, and the qualifier is set to INrange or OUTrange, this command specifies the upper limit of the range for the remote terminal address field.

commands.name: value
commands.description: bus trigger condition is set to COMMAND, and the qualifier is set to LESSthan, MOREthan, EQual, UNEQual, LESSEQual or MOREEQual, this command specifies the value of the 5–bit remote terminal address to be used in the trigger.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_mil1553_b_data_value
commands.description: When the MIL-STD-1553 bus trigger condition is set to DATa, this command specifies the data binary pattern to be used in the trigger.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_mil1553_b_status_address_value
commands.description: When the MIL-STD-1553 bus trigger condition is set to STATus, and the qualifier is set to LESSthan, MOREthan, EQual, UNEQual, LESSEQual or MOREEQual, this command specifies the value of the 5–bit remote terminal address to be used in the trigger.

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_parallel_value
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary data string to be used for a Parallel trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_rs232_c_rx_data_value
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary data string for an RX RS-232 trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_bx_rs232_c_tx_data_value
commands.description: Sets or returns the binary data string for an RS-232 trigger if the trigger condition involves TX

commands.name: trigger_a_bus_source
commands.description: Sets or returns the source for a Serial bus trigger
commands.set: Returns the trigger source, coupling, and slope for the A edge trigger.

commands.name: trigger_a_edge
commands.description: Returns the source, coupling and source for the A edge trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_edge_coupling
commands.description: Sets or returns the type of coupling for the A edge trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_edge_slope
commands.description: Sets or returns the slope for the A edge trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_edge_source
commands.description: Sets or returns the source for the A edge trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_level
commands.description: Sets or returns the trigger level for the A trigger

commands.name: trigger_a_level_auxin
commands.description: Sets or returns the trigger level for the AUXIN port

commands.name: trigger_a_level_chx
commands.description: Specifies or returns the trigger level for the specified trigger channel

commands.name: trigger_a_level_dx
commands.description: Sets or returns the trigger level for the specified digital channel

commands.name: auxin
commands.description: Returns auxiliary input parameters

commands.name: auxin_probe
commands.description: Returns all information concerning the probe attached to auxiliary input

commands.name: auxin_probe_autozero
commands.description: Sets the TekVPI probe attached to the auxiliary input to autozero

commands.name: auxin_probe_command
commands.description: Sets the state of the specified probe control

commands.name: auxin_probe_degauss
commands.description: Starts a degauss/autozero cycle on a TekVPI current probe attached to the auxiliary input

commands.name: auxin_probe_degauss_state
commands.description: Returns the degauss state of the TekVPI current probe attached to the auxiliary input

commands.name: auxin_probe_forcedrange
commands.description: Sets or returns the range of the TekVPI probe attached to the auxiliary input

commands.name: auxin_probe_gain
commands.description: Sets or returns the gain factor of the probe that is attached to the auxiliary input

commands.name: auxin_probe_id_sernumber
commands.description: Returns the serial number of the probe that is attached to the auxiliary input

commands.name: auxin_probe_units
commands.description: Returns the units of measure of the probe that is attached to the auxiliary input

commands.name: chx_bandwidth
commands.description: Sets or returns the bandwidth of the specified channel

commands.name: chx_coupling
commands.description: Sets or returns the coupling setting for the specified channel

commands.name: chx_offset
commands.description: Sets or returns the channel offset

commands.name: chx_position
commands.description: Sets or returns the channel vertical position

commands.name: chx_probe_degauss_state
commands.description: Returns the state of the probe degauss

commands.name: chx_probe_model
commands.description: Sets or returns the probe model for the specified channel

commands.name: chx_probe_propdelay
commands.description: Sets or returns the propagation delay for the probe connected to the specified channel

commands.name: chx_probe_units
commands.description: Returns the units of measure of the probe that is attached to the specified channel

commands.name: chx_scale
commands.description: Sets or returns the vertical scale of the specified channel

commands.name: chx_yunits
commands.description: Sets or returns the units for the specified channel

commands.name: dx_position
commands.description: Sets or returns the vertical position for digital channel<x>

commands.name: refx_horizontal_delay_time
commands.description: Sets or returns the horizontal delay time for reference waveform <x>, where x is the reference channel number. The delay time is expressed in seconds and is limited to ±5 times the reference horizontal scale

commands.name: refx_horizontal_scale
commands.description: Sets or returns the horizontal scale for a reference waveform

commands.name: refx_vertical_position
commands.description: Sets or returns the vertical position of the specified reference waveform

commands.name: refx_vertical_scale
commands.description: Sets or returns the reference waveform vertical scale in vertical units/div

commands.name: select_chx
commands.description: Turns on or off the specified waveform or returns whether the specified channel is on or off

commands.name: curve
commands.description: The CURVe command transfers the waveform data points TO the oscilloscope’s internal reference memory location (REF1–4), which is specified by the DATa:DESTination command. The CURVe? query transfers data FROM the oscilloscope; the source waveform is specified by the DATa:SOUrce command. The first and last data points are specified by the DATa:STARt and DATa:STOP commands. Associated with each waveform transferred using the CURVe command or query is a waveform preamble that provides the data format, scale and associated information needed to interpret the waveform data points. The preamble information for waveforms sent TO the oscilloscope is specified using the WFMInpre commands. The preamble information for waveforms transferred FROM the oscilloscope is specified or queried using the WFMOutpre commands. If the waveform is not displayed, the query form generates an error. The CURVe command and CURVe? query transfer waveform data in ASCII or binary format. ASCII data is sent as a comma-separated list of decimal values. Binary data is sent with the IEEE488.2 binary block header immediately followed by the binary data.

commands.name: data
commands.description: These commands specify the format and location of waveform data that is transferred using the CURVe command, or return the format and location of the waveform data that is transferred with the CURVe? query. You can use the INIT argument to reset all of the DATa parameters to default values. (Note that the *RST and FACtory commands do not reset the DATa parameters.) You can use the SNap argument to automatically set the DATa:STARt and DATa:STOP values to the starting and stopping point of the waveform cursors (if on). Note that setting DATa:STARt and DATa:STOP automatically sets WFMOutpre:NR_Pt.

commands.name: data_destination
commands.description: This command specifies the reference memory location (REF1–4) for storing waveform data transferred into the oscilloscope using the CURVe command.

commands.name: data_encdg
commands.description: This command specifies the encoding format for outgoing waveform data. This command is equivalent to setting WFMOutpre:ENCdg, WFMOutpre:BN_Fmt, and WFMOutpre:BYT_Or. Setting the DATa:ENGdg value causes the corresponding WFMOutpre values to be updated.

commands.name: data_source
commands.description: This command specifies the source waveform to be transferred from the oscilloscope using the CURVe? query. The valid waveform sources are CH1-CH4, MATH, REF1-REF4, D0-D15, and DIGital. Setting DATa:SOUrce automatically constrains the following to valid values for the specified source waveform: WFMOutpre:BYT_Nr, WFMOutpre:BIT_Nr and WFMOutpre:BN_Fmt.

commands.name: data_start
commands.description: This command specifies the starting data point for incoming or outgoing waveform transfer using the CURVe command or query. (Use DATa:STOP to specify the ending data point.) You can set the DATa:STARt and DATa:STOP values automatically to the starting and stopping points of the waveform cursors, if on, using DATa SNap. Note that setting DATa:STARt and DATa:STOP automatically sets WFMOutpre:NR_Pt.

commands.name: data_stop
commands.description: This command specifies the final data point that will be transferred when using the CURVe command or query for incoming or outgoing waveform transfer. (UseDATa:STARt to specify the starting data point.)

commands.name: data_width
commands.description: This command specifies the width, in bytes per point, for waveform data transferred from the scope via the CURVe? query. (This command is synonymous with WFMOutpre:BYT_Nr .) When the source isCH1-CH4, REF1-REF4, MATH or D0-D15, the default width is 1 byte.

commands.name: wavfrm
commands.description: This query returns the waveform preamble and the waveform data for the source waveform specified by the DATa:SOUrce command. This command is equivalent to sending bothWFMOutpre? and CURVe?, with the additional provision that the response to WAVFrm? is guaranteed to provide a synchronized preamble and curve.

commands.name: wfminpre
commands.description: Returns the waveform formatting and scaling specifications to be applied to the next incoming CURVe command data.

commands.name: wfminpre_bit_nr
commands.description: This command specifies the number of bits per data point in the waveform data to be sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVe command. Changing this value also changes the value of WFMInpre:BYT_Nr.

commands.name: wfminpre_bn_fmt
commands.description: This command specifies the format of the data for outgoing waveforms when WFMInpre:ENCdg is set to BINary. The format can either be RI (signed integer) or RP (positive integer).

commands.name: wfminpre_byt_nr
commands.description: This command specifies the number of bytes per data point in the waveform data to be sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVe command. Changing this value also changes the value of WFMInpre:BIT_Nr.

commands.name: wfminpre_byt_or
commands.description: This command specifies which byte of incoming binary waveform data is transmitted first (the byte order). The byte order can either be MSB (most significant byte first) or LSB (least significant byte first, also known as IBM format). This specification only has meaning when WFMInpre:ENCdg is set to BINary and WFMInpre:BYT_Nr is 2.

commands.name: wfminpre_centerfrequency
commands.description: This command specifies the center frequency of an incoming RF trace. This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored, and the query always returns 0.0000.

commands.name: wfminpre_domain
commands.description: This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored and the query always returns TIMe.

commands.name: wfminpre_encdg
commands.description: This command specifies the type of encoding of the incoming waveform data to be sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVe command. Supported types are BINary and ASCii.

commands.name: wfminpre_nr_pt
commands.description: This command specifies the number of data points that are in the incoming waveform record to be sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVe command.

commands.name: wfminpre_pt_fmt
commands.description: This command specifies the format of the data points to be sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVE command. This can be Y for YT format, or ENV for envelope format (min/max pairs). Regardless of the argument used, the scale, offset, and so on are interpreted similarly. When ENV is used, waveform data is interpreted as min-max pairs (the minimum value precedes the maximum for each pair); when Y is used, it is interpreted over a single point.

commands.name: wfminpre_pt_off
commands.description: The set form of this command is ignored. The query form always returns a 0.

commands.name: wfminpre_reflevel
commands.description: This command specifies the Reference Level of the incoming waveform. This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored, and the query always returns 0.0000.

commands.name: wfminpre_span
commands.description: This command specifies the frequency span of an incoming RF trace. This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored, and the query always returns 0.0000.

commands.name: wfminpre_wfmtype
commands.description: This command specifies the type of waveform that is being transferred to the oscilloscope for storage in one of the REF1 — REF4 memory locations. This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored and the query always returns ANALOG.

commands.name: wfminpre_xincr
commands.description: This command specifies the horizontal interval between incoming waveform points sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVE command. The units can be time, in seconds, or frequency, in hertz, and can be specified or queried using the WFMInpre:XUNit command.

commands.name: wfminpre_xunit
commands.description: This command specifies the horizontal units of the x-axis of the data points being sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVE command. This value can be in “s” or “Hz”.

commands.name: wfminpre_xzero
commands.description: This command specifies the position value of the first data point in the incoming waveform record being sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVE command. The units are determined or queried using the WFMInpre:XUNit command and are typically time, in seconds, or frequency, in hertz. This time or frequency is relative to the time or frequency of the trigger, which is always 0. Thus, the XZEro value can be negative.

commands.name: wfminpre_ymult
commands.description: This command specifies the vertical scale multiplying factor to be used to convert the incoming data point values being sent to the oscilloscope, from digitizing levels into the units specified by the WFMInpre:YUNit command. For one byte waveform data, there are 256 digitizing levels. For two byte waveform data there are 65,536 digitizing levels.

commands.name: wfminpre_yoff
commands.description: This command specifies the vertical position of the destination reference waveform in digitizing levels. There are 25 digitizing levels per vertical division for 1-byte data, and 6400 digitizing levels per vertical division for 2-byte data. Variations in this number are analogous to changing the vertical position of the waveform.

commands.name: wfminpre_yunit
commands.description: This command specifies the vertical units of data points in the incoming waveform record sent to the oscilloscope using the CURVE command. This can be any of several string values, depending upon the vertical units of the waveform being sent.

commands.name: wfminpre_yzero
commands.description: This command specifies the vertical offset of the destination reference waveform in units specified by the WFMInpre:YUNit command. Variations in this number are analogous to changing the vertical offset of the waveform. The WFMInpre:YMUlt, WFMInpre:YOFf, and WFMInpre:YZEro commands are used to convert waveform record values to units specified using the WFMInpre:YUNit command (YUNit units).

commands.name: wfmoutpre
commands.description: This query returns the information needed to interpret the waveform data points returned by the CURVe? query. It returns the waveform transmission and formatting parameters for the waveform specified by the DATa:SOUrce command.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_bit_nr
commands.description: This command specifies the number of bits per data point in the outgoing waveform being transferred using the CURVe? query. Changing the value of WFMOutpre:BIT_Nr also changes the values of WFMOutpre:BYT_Or and DATa:WIDth.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_bn_fmt
commands.description: This command specifies the format of the binary data for outgoing waveforms when WFMOutpre:ENCdg is set to BINary. The format can be RI (signed integer) or RP (positive integer) for analog channels. Changing the value of WFMOutpre:BN_Fmt also changes the value of DATa:ENCdg. The waveform is specified by the DATa:SOUrce command.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_byt_nr
commands.description: This command specifies the data width for the outgoing waveform specified by the DATa:SOUrce command. (This command is synonymous withDATa:WIDTH.) Note that changing WFMOutpre:BYT_Nr also changes WFMOutpre:BIT_Nr and DATa:WIDth.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_byt_or
commands.description: This command specifies which byte of outgoing binary waveform data is transmitted first (the byte order). The byte order can either be MSB (most significant byte first) or LSB (least significant byte first, also known as IBM format). This specification only has meaning when WFMOutpre:ENCdg is set to BINary and WFMOutpre:BYT_Nr is 2.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_centerfrequency
commands.description: This query returns the center frequency of an incoming waveform. This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored, and the query always returns 0.0000.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_domain
commands.description: This query is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only and always returns TIMe.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_encdg
commands.description: This command specifies the type of encoding (BINary or ASCii) of the outgoing waveform data queried using the CURVe? query. (This can also be set using the DATa:ENCdg command, which provides the ability to set WFMOutpre:ENCdg, WFMOutpre:BN_Fmt, and WFMOutpre:BYT_Or using a single command.)

commands.name: wfmoutpre_nr_pt
commands.description: This query returns the number of data points in the waveform record that will be transmitted in response to a CURVe? query. This value is the adjusted range specified by DATA:START and DATA:STOP commands. Note that the oscilloscope automatically adjusts the DATA:START and DATA:STOP values when the DATA:STOP value is less than the DATA:START value, and when the DATA:START and/or DATA:STOP value is greater than the record length of the source waveform. The adjusted DATA:START and DATA:STOP values determine WFMOUTPRE:NR_PT. (You can use DATa:STARt and DATa:STOP to transfer partial waveforms.) If the waveform specified by the DATa:SOUrce command is not turned on, an error will be generated.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_pt_fmt
commands.description: This query returns the point format of the data points in the outgoing waveform record transferred using the CURVe? query. The returned values can be Y, which indicates normal waveform points for YT format, or ENV, which indicates envelope mode format in which the data is returned as a series of min/max pairs. The minimum value precedes the maximum. The outgoing waveform is specified by the DATa:SOUrce command. The query command will time out and an error will be generated if the waveform specified by DATa:SOUrce is not turned on.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_pt_off
commands.description: This query always returns 0 if the outgoing waveform specified by DATA:SOUrce is on or displayed.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_pt_order
commands.description: This query returns the point ordering, which is always linear.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_reflevel
commands.description: This query returns the Reference Level of an outgoing waveform. This query is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only and always returns 0.0000.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_span
commands.description: This query returns the frequency span of the outgoing waveform. This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored, and the query always returns 0.0000.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_wfid
commands.description: This query returns a string that describes several aspects of the acquisition parameters for the source waveform, including Source, Coupling, Vertical Scale, Horizontal Scale, Record Length and Acquisition Mode. If the waveform specified by DATa:SOUrce command is not turned on, an error will be generated.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_wfmtype
commands.description: This query returns the type of the outgoing waveform. This command is provided for waveform transfer compatibility with mixed domain oscilloscopes only. The command form is ignored, and the query always returns ANALOG.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_xincr
commands.description: This query returns the horizontal point spacing in units of time (seconds), or frequency (hertz) between data points in the waveform record transferred using the CURVe? query. This value corresponds to the sampling interval.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_xunit
commands.description: This query indicates the horizontal units of the x-axis of the waveform record transferred using the CURVe? query. Typically, this value is "s" when the waveform source is displayed in the time domain, and "Hz" when the waveform source is displayed in the frequency domain. When the waveform source is Math or a reference waveform, the value can be "s" or "Hz".

commands.name: wfmoutpre_xzero
commands.description: This query returns the time coordinate, in seconds, or frequency, in hertz, of the first data point in the outgoing waveform record transferred using the CURVe? query. This time or frequency is relative to the time of the trigger, which is always 0. Thus, the XZEro time or frequency can be negative.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_ymult
commands.description: This query returns the vertical scale multiplying factor used to convert the waveform data point values in the outgoing waveform record from digitizing levels to the YUNit units. You can determine the units by using the WFMOutpre:YUNit query. See the description of the WFMInpre:YMUlt command to see how this scale factor is used to convert waveform sample values to volts.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_yoff
commands.description: This query returns the vertical position of the source waveform in digitizing levels. There are 25 digitizing levels per vertical division for 1-byte data, and 6400 digitizing levels per vertical division for 2-byte data. See the description of WFMInpre:YOFf to see how this position is used to convert waveform sample values to volts.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_yunit
commands.description: This query returns the units of data points in the outgoing waveform record transferred using the CURVe? query. This can be any of several string values, depending upon the vertical units of the source waveform (specified by the DATa:SOUrce command). Typically, this is “V” for volts.

commands.name: wfmoutpre_yzero
commands.description: This query returns the vertical offset of the source waveform. You can determine the units using the WFMOutpre:YUNit? query. See the description of WFMInpre:YZEro to see how this offset is used to convert waveform sample values to volts.

commands.name: zoom
commands.description: Returns the current horizontal positioning and scaling of the zoom display

commands.name: zoom_mode
commands.description: Sets or returns the zoom mode
examples: ZOOM:MODE OFF turns off Zoom mode

commands.name: zoom_zoomx
commands.description: Returns the current horizontal positioning and scaling of the display. <x> can only be 1

commands.name: zoom_zoomx_factor
commands.description: Returns the zoom factor of the zoom window. <x> can only be 1

commands.name: zoom_zoomx_position
commands.description: Sets or returns the horizontal position of the zoom window, in terms of 0 to 100.0% of the zoom window. <x> can only be 1

commands.name: zoom_zoomx_scale
commands.description: Sets or returns the horizontal scale of the zoom window. <x> can only be 1

commands.name: zoom_zoomx_state
commands.description: Specifies or returns a trace as zoomed, on or off. <x> can only be 1
